返回本批次

Servo 现已在 crates.io 上提供

Servo is now available on crates.io

servo.org 作者 ffin 分数 445 评论 141 2026/04/13 20:12
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 初始 crates.io 版本和 Servo 的 LTS 版本发布于 2026-04-13 今天，Servo 团队发布了 Servo crate v0.1.0。这是我们第一个 crates.io 版本的伺服箱，它允许将 Servo 用作库。我们目前没有任何计划将演示浏览器servoshell 发布到crates.io。自 2025 年 10 月首次在 GitHub 发布以来的 5 个版本中，我们的发布流程已经成熟，主要的“瓶颈”现在是人工撰写的每月博客文章。由于我们对此版本感到非常兴奋，因此我们决定不等待每月博客文章完成，而是承诺在未来几周内提供每月更新。从版本号可以看出，这个版本不是1.0版本。事实上，我们还没有讨论完 1.0 对 Servo 意味着什么。尽管如此，版本号的增加反映出我们对 Servo 的嵌入 API 及其满足某些用户需求的能力越来越有信心。与此同时，我们还决定提供 Servo 的长期支持 (LTS) 版本，因为预计每月定期发布会发生重大变化，并且一些嵌入者可能更喜欢按计划每半年进行一次重大升级，同时仍然接收安全更新和（希望如此！）一些迁移指南。有关 LTS 版本的更多详细信息，请参阅 Servo 书中的相应部分。

Original Summary

  1. Initial crates.io release and LTS version of Servo Posted 2026-04-13 Today the Servo team has released v0.1.0 of the servo crate. This is our first crates.io release of the servo crate that allows Servo to be used as a library. We currently do not have any plans of publishing our demo browser servoshell to crates.io. In the 5 releases since our initial GitHub release in October 2025, our release process has matured, with the main “bottleneck” now being the human-written monthly blog post. Since we’re quite excited about this release, we decided to not wait for the monthly blog post to be finished, but promise to deliver the monthly update in the coming weeks. As you can see from the version number, this release is not a 1.0 release. In fact, we still haven’t finished discussing what 1.0 means for Servo. Nevertheless, the increased version number reflects our growing confidence in Servo’s embedding API and its ability to meet some users’ needs. In the meantime we also decided to offer a long-term support (LTS) version of Servo, since breaking changes in the regular monthly releases are expected and some embedders might prefer doing major upgrades on a scheduled half-yearly basis while still receiving security updates and (hopefully!) some migration guides. For more details on the LTS release, see the respective section in the Servo book.

评论（默认中文）

nicoburns 2026/04/13 21:47
一些注意事项：

- docs.rs 文档仍在构建中，但最近 RC 中的文档可用 [0]

- Slint 项目有一个将 Servo 嵌入 Slint 的示例 [1]，这是如何使用嵌入 API 的好示例，并且应该相对容易适应使用 wgpu 渲染的任何其他 GUI 框架。

- Stylo [2] 和 WebRender [3] 都已发布到 crates.io，并且可以独立使用（Stylo 实际上已经每月发布约一年的版本，但我们从未真正公开过这一点）。

- 计划按月节奏持续发布

[0]: https://docs.rs/servo/0.1.0-rc2/servo

[1]： https://github.com/slint-ui/slint/tree/master/examples/servo

[2]： https://docs.rs/stylo

[3]： https://docs.rs/webrender

Some notes:

- The docs.rs docs are still building, but the docs from the recent RC are available [0]

- The Slint project have an example of embedding Servo into Slint [1] which is good example of how to use the embedding API, and should be relatively easy to adapt to any other GUI framework which renders using wgpu.

- Stylo [2] and WebRender [3] have both also been published to crates.io, and can be useful standalone (Stylo has actually been getting monthly releases for ~year but we never really publicised that).

- Ongoing releases on a monthly cadance are planned

[0]: https://docs.rs/servo/0.1.0-rc2/servo

[1]: https://github.com/slint-ui/slint/tree/master/examples/servo

[2]: https://docs.rs/stylo

[3]: https://docs.rs/webrender

Tmpod 2026/04/14 04:31

simonw 2026/04/13 23:11
这是一个 vivi 编码的“servo-shot”CLI 工具，它使用此箱来渲染网页的图像： https://github.com/simonw/research/tree/main/servo-crate-exp...

  git clone https://github.com/simonw/research
  cd research/servo-crate-exploration/servo-shot
  cargo build
  ./target/debug/servo-shot https://news.ycombinator.com/
这是它生成的图像： https://gist.github.com/simonw/c2cb4fcb15b0837bbc4540c3d398c...
Here's a vibe-coded "servo-shot" CLI tool which uses this crate to render an image of a web page: https://github.com/simonw/research/tree/main/servo-crate-exp...

  git clone https://github.com/simonw/research
  cd research/servo-crate-exploration/servo-shot
  cargo build
  ./target/debug/servo-shot https://news.ycombinator.com/
Here's the image it generated: https://gist.github.com/simonw/c2cb4fcb15b0837bbc4540c3d398c...

mrbonner 2026/04/14 05:04
凉爽的！我检查了源代码，注意到即使是 LLM 也更喜欢简化的高级 Rust 编码风格：使用诸如 String 之类的值类型，使用诸如引用计数之类的智能指针，自由克隆等……而不是与借用检查器看门人对抗。

这是我更喜欢使用 Rust 的风格。来自 Python、Typescript 甚至 Java，即使使用这种高水平的 Rust，它也已经产生了令人难以置信的改进。

Cool! I checked the source and noticed that even LLM prefers simplified, high level Rust coding styles: use value types such as String, use smart pointers such as reference counting, clone liberally, etc… instead of fighting the borrow checker gatekeepers.

It is the style I prefer to use Rust for. Coming from Python, Typescript and even Java, even with this high level Rust, it yields incredible improvement already.

在 HN 中查看
minimaxir 2026/04/13 23:42
我一直在构建/vibecoding一个类似的工具，不幸的是得出的结论是，在实践中，有太多的功能依赖于完整的Chrome堆栈，尽管文件大小，但使用真正的Chromium安装更为务实。不过，性能/图像生成速度仍然很好。

在 Rust 中，氧化铬箱是一种与其交互以获取屏幕截图的高性能方式： https://crates.io/crates/chromiumoxide

I have been building/vibecoding a similar tool and unfortunately came to the conclusion that in practice, there are just too many features dependent on the full Chrome stack that it's just more pragmatic to use a real Chromium installation despite the file size. Performance/image generation speed is still fine, though.

In Rust, the chromiumoxide crate is a performant way to interface with it for screenshots: https://crates.io/crates/chromiumoxide

rafaelmn 2026/04/13 23:22
这应该是人工智能编码技能的真正基准——我们以多快的速度获得每个人都同意我们需要但没有人能够资助开发的安全/现代基础设施/工具。

如果 Anthropic 希望在不发布 Mythos 的情况下对其进行营销 - 向我们展示伺服贡献日志或类似内容。它与他们的基本基础设施安全目标非常吻合。

我相信这样的话，y 板凳上的增幅会超过 x%。

雇用 Servo 或 Rust 的核心贡献者，给他无限的模型访问权限，让我们看看每个版本能走多远。

This should be the real benchmark of AI coding skills - how fast do we get safe/modern infrastructure/tooling that everyone agrees we need but nobody can fund the development.

If Anthropic wants marketing for Mythos without publishing it - show us servo contrib log or something like that. It aligns nicely with their fundamental infrastructure safety goals.

I'd trust that way more than x% increase on y bench.

Hire a core contributor on Servo or Rust, give him unlimited model access and let's see how far we get with each release.

falcor84 2026/04/13 23:31
在我看来，重点不应该是编码，而应该是测试，特别是安全评估。特别是对于关键基础设施，我希望我们有一种非常可靠的测试方法，无论谁/什么编写了代码都无关紧要。
As I see it, the focus should not be about the coding, but about the testing, and particularly the security evaluation. Particularly for critical infrastructure, I would want us to have a testing approach that is so reliable that it wouldn't matter who/what wrote the code.
在 HN 中查看
jbvlkt 2026/04/14 05:09
我最近一直在思考这个问题，测试和安全评估难道不是比设计和仔细实现新功能更困难的问题吗？我认为 vivicoding 自动化了软件开发中最简单的步骤，同时使更具挑战性/昂贵的步骤变得更加困难。如果没有人理解代码，我们该如何调试关键基础设施中的复杂问题呢？未来代理商有可能能够做到这一点，但我觉得我们还没有做到这一点。
I have been thinking about that lately and isn't testing and security evaluation way harder problem than designing and carefully implementing new features? I think that vibecoding automates easiest step in SW development while making more challenging/expensive steps harder. How are we suppose to debug complex problems in critical infrastructure if no one understands code? It is possible that in future agents will be able to do that but it feels to me that we are not there yet.
在 HN 中查看
bawolff 2026/04/13 23:49
我认为这永远不可能。

在某些时候，安全性就变成了——程序做了人类想要它做的事情，但没有意识到他们实际上并不想要它。

由于规范不正确，无论进行多少测试都无法修复逻辑错误。

I dont think that will ever be possible.

At some point security becomes - the program does the thing the human wanted it to do but didn't realize they didn't actually want.

No amount of testing can fix logic bugs due to bad specification.

在 HN 中查看
skrtskrt 2026/04/14 01:25
人工智能作为高级模糊测试非常有用——在这种高级系统中几乎没有任何错误是规范逻辑错误。它是基于低级安全的东西，寻找对本地进程进行 DDOS 的方法，或者解决操作系统级安全限制等。
AI as advanced fuzz-testing is ridiculously helpful though - hardly any bug you can in this sort of advanced system is a specification logic bug. It's low-level security-based stuff, finding ways to DDOS a local process, or work around OS-level security restrictions, etc.
在 HN 中查看
bawolff 2026/04/14 03:25
我有点怀疑人工智能在模糊测试方面是否那么出色。抛开这些不谈，我们在这里讨论的是网络浏览器。不良规范或误解规范导致的安全问题相对常见。
I'm kind of doubtful that AI is all that great at fuzz testing. Putting that aside though, we are talking about web browsers here. Security issues from bad specification or misunderstanding the specification is relatively common.

px43 2026/04/13 23:56

我确信我们将拥有活跃的基础设施和缓慢的基础设施，其中之一会更频繁地烧毁。只有时间才能告诉我们谁能在猛攻中幸存下来，谁会被淘汰，但我个人不会在缓慢的基础设施上下任何赌注。

That's just status quo, which isn't really holding up in the modern era IMO.

I'm sure we'll have vibed infrastructure and slow infrastructure, and one of them will burn down more frequently. Only time will tell who survives the onslaught and who gets dropped, but I personally won't be making any bets on slow infrastructure.

falcor84 2026/04/14 00:18
我有些同意，但即便如此，我仍认为这种理解的正确级别应该是架构和数据流不变量级别，而不是代码本身。实际上，这些可以很好地执行，作为针对人类编写的图表规范的测试。
I somewhat agree, but even then would argue that the proper level at which this understanding should reside is at the architecture and data flow invariants levels, rather than the code itself. And these can actually be enforced quite well as tests against human-authored diagrammatical specs.

t43562 2026/04/14 00:38
如果您不完全理解代码，您如何知道它准确地实现了您的架构，并且没有以您没有想到的方式进行？

作为一个简单的例子，我刚刚在几周前生成的一些代码中发现了一些不相关的废话。它在简单的情况下有效，这就是为什么我从未发现它，但在更复杂的情况下会产生一些奇怪的效果。也许是我的提示解释得不够好，但我怎么知道我在不阅读代码的情况下失败了呢？

If you don't fully understand the code how do you know it implements your architecture exactly and without doing it in a way that has implications you hadn't thought of?

As a trivial example I just found a piece of irrelevant crap in some code I generated a couple of weeks ago. It worked in the simple cases which is why I never spotted it but would have had some weird effects in more complicated ones. It was my prompting that didn't explain well enough perhaps but how was I to know I failed without reading the code?

在 HN 中查看
rl3 2026/04/14 06:31
>> ...给他无限的模型访问权限

>我们不需要振动编码的关键基础设施。

我认为，当您拥有几乎无限的计算能力时，它能够真正将测试编写和代码审查锁定到正常氛围代码设置和预算无法实现的程度。

也就是说，对于真正关键的事情，我可以看到对给定的生成代码片段进行最终的人工审查步骤，然后是硬锁。如果该工作流程还没有流行的话，它也会流行起来。

>> ...give him unlimited model access

>We do not need vibe-coded critical infrastructure.

I think when you have virtually unlimited compute, it affords the ability to really lock down test writing and code review to a degree that isn't possible with normal vibe code setups and budgets.

That said for truly critical things, I could see a final human review step for a given piece of generated code, followed by a hard lock. That workflow is going to be popular if it already isn't.

在 HN 中查看
mort96 2026/04/14 06:45
计算的可用性或缺乏与我的观点完全无关。更多的氛围编码测试并不能解决问题。
The availability or lack thereof of compute has absolutely nothing to do with my opinion. More vibe coded tests doesn't fix the problem.
在 HN 中查看
rl3 2026/04/14 06:52
当某个功能有 50 个不同的模型对其进行审查时，可能会出现这种情况，每个模型可能会审查多次。

也许是该功能的复杂审查链的一部分，总共有数百次 LLM 调用。

只要最后有人审查它并且它被锁定，我就认为它 最终 代码最初是如何创建的并不重要。

在达到这一点之前，有很多原因很重要，但更多的是与系统设计问题有关。当然，您也可以认为安全是一个持续的过程，部分源于系统设计，您不会错。

我突然想到这里有一些最近的现有技术：

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47721953

可以公平地说，Linux 内核是至关重要的基础设施，或者至少是其中的一个组成部分。

It might when an individual function has 50 different models reviewing it, potentially multiple times each.

Perhaps part of a complex review chain for said function that's a few hundred LLM invocations total.

So long as there's a human reviewing it at the end and it gets locked, I'd argue it ultimately doesn't matter how the code was initially created.

There's a lot of reasons it would matter before it gets to that point, just more to do with system design concerns. Of course, you could also argue safety is an ongoing process that partially derives from system design and you wouldn't be wrong.

It occurred to me there's some recent prior art here:

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47721953

It's probably fair to say the Linux kernel is critical infra, or at least a component piece in a lot of it.

在 HN 中查看
Night_Thastus 2026/04/14 02:54
这种基础设施的问题不在于最初的开发开销。

这是维护。长期的、缓慢的、无趣的工作必须不断地完成。需要有人长期支持它，否则它永远不会被采用和广泛使用。

至少现在，法学硕士在这方面并不擅长。它们非常适合快速 创造 较小的项目。这些项目越老、越大，它们的效果就越差。

The problem with such infrastructure is not the initial development overhead.

It's the maintenance. The long term, slow burn, uninteresting work that must be done continually. Someone needs to be behind it for the long haul or it will never get adopted and used widely.

Right now, at least, LLMs are not great at that. They're great for quickly creating smaller projects. They get less good the older and larger those projects get.

rafaelmn 2026/04/14 03:08
我的意思是，下一代模型在更大的背景下执行得越来越好。我发现 GPT 5.4 xhigh 即使在较大的代码库上也能出色地进行分析。

https://x.com/mitchellh/status/2029348087538565612

像这样的东西，这些模型是导致不平凡的大规模错误的根本原因，SOTA 中已经存在了。

如果下一代模型能够解决这些更高的可靠性并更好地实现它们，我不会感到惊讶。到那时就已经是足够好的维护者了。

他们建议新模型可以将多个新发现的漏洞链接到 RCE 和权限升级等中。如果没有更大范围的规划/理解，你就无法做到这一点，而且不可靠。

https://x.com/mitchellh/status/2029348087538565612

Stuff like this where these models are root causing nontrivial large scale bugs is already there in SOTA.

I would not be surprised if next generation models can both resolve those more reliability and implement them better. At that point would be sufficiently good maintainers.

They are suggesting that new models can chain multiple newly discovered vulnerabilities into RCE and privilege escalations etc. You can't do this without larger scope planning/understanding, not reliabily.

[1] https://crates.io/crates/typst

okanat 2026/04/14 04:05
Typst 相当于 Rust 之于 C++ 但之于 Latex。更健全的语法，经过深思熟虑的可扩展性（包括脚本和宏）， 理智的表，一个包管理器。我很高兴我出于文档目的转而使用它。我也期待着用它来编译网页。
Typst is what Rust is to C++ but for Latex. Saner syntax, well thought extensibility (including scripting and macros), tables that are sane, a package manager. I am happy that I switched to it for documentation purposes. I am looking forward to compiling web pages with it too.
在 HN 中查看
globular-toast 2026/04/14 04:25
我最近部署了 Typest 来自动生成 PDF 信件。由于熟悉 (La)TeX（我排版了从信件到博士论文的所有内容），我对它的速度感到震惊。它足够快，可以在 HTTP 请求周期中使用。该语言也非常容易学习。

不确定它在排版方面是否与 TeX 一样好，但看起来已经足够好了。当我做论文的时候，TikZ 就更有价值了。我不知道是否有任何替代品。

I recently deployed typst to generate PDF letters automatically. Being familiar with (La)TeX (I've typeset everything from letters to my PhD thesis), I was shocked at the speed. It's quick enough to use in an HTTP request cycle. The language was also super easy to learn.

Not sure if it's quite as good as TeX at typesetting, but it seems good enough. When I did my thesis, TikZ was even more valuable. I don't know if there's any replacement for that.

swiftcoder 2026/04/14 00:05
具体来说，它是 Mozilla 早期开发基于 Rust 的浏览器的浏览器引擎，也是整个 Rust 生态系统的激励项目之一
Specifically, it's the browser engine that spun out of Mozilla's early efforts towards a rust-based browser, and is one of the motivating projects for the entire Rust ecosystem
在 HN 中查看
01HNNWZ0MV43FF 2026/04/14 03:13
是的，Servo 是一个可嵌入的 Web 浏览器/WebView，如 Chromium 嵌入式框架。 （持续教育基金）

Electron = Node.js + CEF

Tauri = Rust + webview

Tauri 有一个实验分支，使用 Servo 提供捆绑的 Web 视图。目前它依赖于系统级 webview，例如 Windows 上的 Edge、MacOS 上的 Safari 和 Linux 上的 webkit-gtk。

Yes, Servo is an embeddable web browser / webview, like Chromium Embedded Framework. (CEF)

Electron = Node.js + CEF

Tauri = Rust + webview

Tauri has an experimental branch to use Servo to provide a bundled webview. Currently it relies on a system-level webview, like Edge on Windows, Safari on MacOS, and webkit-gtk on Linux.

在 HN 中查看
mort96 2026/04/13 22:55
Rust 版本控制的根本问题是 0.3.5 兼容 0.3.6，但不兼容 0.4.0 或 1.0.0；当主要版本为0时，次要版本充当主要版本，补丁充当次要版本。因此，包会迭代 0.x 版本，最终达到“稳定”的版本。

如果版本 0.7 被证明符合正确的 API 并且不需要向后不兼容的更改，那么发布版本 1.0 将与用户的主要版本更改一样具有破坏性，并通过版本语义传达这是一个重大更改。

Semver 声明 0.x 版本用于初始开发，根本没有稳定性保证。这是版本控制系统的正确语义，但 Cargo 不遵循 semver 的这一部分。在整个 0.x 周期中提供稳定性保证不可避免地会导致项目陷入 0.x 困境。

这是我对 Cargo 最大的抱怨之一。但 Rust 人们似乎普遍认为这不是问题，所以我认为它永远不会得到解决。

The fundamental problem with Rust versioning is that 0.3.5 is compatible with 0.3.6, but not 0.4.0 or 1.0.0; when major version is 0, the minor takes the role of major and patch takes the role of minor. So packages iterate through 0.x versions, and eventually, they reach a version that's "stable".

If version 0.7 turned out to hit the right API and not require backward incompatible changes, releasing a version 1.0 would be as disruptive as a major version change to your users and communicate through version semantics that it is a breaking change.

Semver declares that version 0.x is for initial development where there is no stability guarantee at all. This is the right semantics for a versioning system, but Cargo doesn't follow this part of semver. Providing stability guarantees throughout the 0.x cycle inevitably results in projects getting stuck in 0.x.

This is one of my biggest gripes with Cargo. But Rust people seem to universally consider it a non-issue so I don't think it'll ever be fixed.

在 HN 中查看
kibwen 2026/04/14 03:11
> 如果版本 0.7 被证明符合正确的 API 并且不需要向后不兼容的更改，则发布版本 1.0 将与主要版本更改一样具有破坏性

不，这就是 semver 技巧的用途： https://github.com/dtolnay/semver-trick

TL;DR：您采用 0.7 库，将其发布为 1.0，然后制作一个 0.7.1 版本，该版本除了依赖 1.0 之外什么也不做，并重新导出其所有项目。 Tada，一个兼容的 1.0 版本，0.7 用户升级时将自动获得。

更有趣的是，您可以使用它来协调仅部分破坏的更改，例如如果您的库中有 100 个 API，但只对其中一个进行了重大更改，则您可以重新导出 99 个未损坏的 API，并且最终只会在实践中为实际使用具有重大更改的 API 的用户进行重大更改。

> If version 0.7 turned out to hit the right API and not require backward incompatible changes, releasing a version 1.0 would be as disruptive as a major version change

Nope, this is what the semver trick is for: https://github.com/dtolnay/semver-trick

TL;DR: You take the 0.7 library, release it as 1.0, then make a 0.7.1 release that does nothing other than depend on 1.0 and re-export all its items. Tada, a compatible 1.0 release that 0.7 users will get automatically when they upgrade.

Even more interesting is that you can use this to coordinate only partially-breaking changes, e.g. if you have 100 APIs in your library but only make a breaking change to one, you can re-export the 99 unbroken APIs and only end up making breaking changes in practice for users who actually use the one API with breaking changes.

在 HN 中查看
sheepscreek 2026/04/13 23:05
> Rust 版本控制的根本问题是 0.3.5 兼容 0.3.6，但不兼容 0.4.0 或 1.0.0

这是 semver 的一个功能，而不是一个 bug :)

长答案：您注意到主要版本中的次要版本可以引入新的 API 和更改，但一般来说，在下一个主要版本之前不应破坏现有的 API，这是正确的。

然而，此规则仅适用于图书馆 后 他们达到了 1.0.0。在 1.0.0 之前，人们不应该期望任何 API 会被真正冻结。

> The fundamental problem with Rust versioning is that 0.3.5 is compatible with 0.3.6, but not 0.4.0 or 1.0.0

That’s a feature of semver, not a bug :)

Long answer: You are right to notice that minor versions within a major release can introduce new APIs and changes but generally, should not break existing APIs until the next major release.

However, this rule only applies to libraries after they reach 1.0.0. Before 1.0.0, one shouldn’t expect any APIs to be frozen really.

在 HN 中查看
mort96 2026/04/13 23:10
不，显然不是。塞姆弗 说：

> 主要版本零 (0.y.z) 用于初始开发。任何事情都可能随时改变。公共 API 不应该被认为是稳定的。

Cargo 通过考虑 0.3.5 与 0.3.6 兼容来明确打破 Semver。

No, it's explicitly not. Semver says:

> Major version zero (0.y.z) is for initial development. Anything MAY change at any time. The public API SHOULD NOT be considered stable.

Cargo is explicitly breaking with Semver by considering 0.3.5 compatible with 0.3.6.

在 HN 中查看
demurgos 2026/04/13 23:45
更进一步，semver 提供了语义和排序，但它没有提及版本要求语法。描述一系列版本的插入符运算符不是规范的一部分。它是由最初支持 semver 的包管理器（例如 npm 或 gem）引入的。 Cargo 决定默认使用插入符号运算符，但它仍然是插入符号运算符。

实际上，使用第一个非零组件来定义 API 兼容版本组并没有真正的问题，并且大多数包管理器都同意语义。

To go further, semver provides semantics and an ordering but it says nothing about version requirement syntax. The caret operator to describe a range of versions is not part of the spec. It was introduced by initial semver-aware package managers such as npm or gem. Cargo decided to default to the caret operator, but it's still the caret operator.

In practice, there's no real issue with using the first non-zero component to define the group of API-compatible releases and most package managers agree on the semantics.

在 HN 中查看
Starlevel004 2026/04/13 23:19
标准库有一大堆工具可以让他们通过必需的选择来测试和发展 API，但每个生态系统包都必须首先尝试正确，因为 Cargo 会默默地强制更新包，而这些进化工具不可用于第三方包。

如此愚蠢的状况。

The standard library has a whole bunch of tools to let them test and evolve APIs with a required-opt in, but every single ecosystem package has to get it right first try because Cargo will silently forcibly update packages and those evolution tools aren't available to third party packages.

Such a stupid state of affairs.

在 HN 中查看
moron4hire 2026/04/13 23:05
就我个人而言，我认为0主要版本是一个坏主意。我听到人们希望不想在开发的早期阶段保证稳定性，并且不希望人们依赖它。但是将其隐藏在“v0.x”后面并不会改变您的事实 是 发布版本和人员 是 取决于它。

如果您不希望人们依赖您的包（因此有“依赖”一词），那么为什么要发布它呢？如果您的公共界面发生变化，请更改主要版本号。你怕什么？人们认真对待你的项目吗？

Personally, I think the 0 major version is a bad idea. I hear the desire to not want to have to make guarantees about stability in the early stages of development and you don't want people depending on it. But hiding that behind "v0.x" doesn't change the fact that you are releasing versions and people are depending on it.

If you didn't want people to depend on your package (hence the word "dependency") then why release it? If your public interface changes, bump that major version number. What are you afraid of? People taking your project seriously?

在 HN 中查看
jaapz 2026/04/13 23:19
0.x 并不是你不希望人们依赖它，你只是不希望他们在你快速引入一些重大更改时来抱怨。该项目仍在开发中，它可能足够稳定，可以在“真实项目（tm）”中使用，但它也可能仍然会发生重大变化。由用户决定他们是否同意这一点。

1.x 表明（至少对我来说）您对包的当前状态非常满意，并且未来不会看到任何重大的重大变化。当 2.x 出现时，通常是在 1.x 使用很长时间之后，人们提出了一些痛点，这些痛点只能通过打破 API 来解决。

0.x is not that you don't want people depending on it, you just don't want them to come and complain when you quickly introduce some breaking changes. The project is still in development, it might be stable enough for use in "real projects(tm)", but it might also still significantly change. It is up to the user to decide whether they are OK with this.

1.x communicates (to me at least) you are pretty happy with the current state of the package and don't see any considerable breaking changes in the future. When 2.x comes around, this is often after 1.x has been in use for a long time and people have raised some pain points that can only be addressed by breaking the API.

OtomotO 2026/04/13 23:46
但人们会抱怨，所以 ex falso quodlibet
But people will complain, so ex falso quodlibet
在 HN 中查看
moron4hire 2026/04/13 23:23
如果其他人可以使用您的软件，那么您应该使用 v1.1 版本。如果您还没有准备好使用 v1，那么您不应该发布给其他人。

因为这个评论，“该项目仍在开发中，它可能足够稳定，可以在“真实项目（tm）”中使用，但它也可能仍然会发生重大变化。”这描述了每个项目。每个项目总是在发展中。每个项目都是稳定的，直到它不稳定为止。如果不是，你就增加主号码。

If you are at the point that other people can use your software, then you should use v1. If you are not ready for v1, then you shouldn't be releasing to other people.

Because this comment, "The project is still in development, it might be stable enough for use in "real projects(tm)", but it might also still significantly change." That describes every project. Every project is always in development. Every project is stable until it isn't. And when it isn't, you bump the major number.

the__alchemist 2026/04/13 23:54
我认为我们可以找出为什么每次重大更改都增加版本号也不是一个优雅的解决方案的原因：您最终会得到数百或数千个版本号。
I think we can come up with a reason why bumping the version number each breaking change isn't an elegant solution either: You would end up with version numbers in the hundreds or thousands.

zokier 2026/04/14 02:47
浏览器版本号有几百个，似乎不成问题。

在 HN 中查看
the__alchemist 2026/04/14 02:55
的确！我认为基于 0 的版本控制以及我提出的这个（也许？）缺点解决了想要限制破坏性更改所造成的损害与保留进行更改的能力之间的紧张关系。
Indeed! I think both 0-based versioning, and this (maybe?) downside I bring up addresses the tension between wanting to limit the damage caused by breaking changes with retaining the ability to make them.
在 HN 中查看
mort96 2026/04/13 23:13
版本控制就是沟通。我发现通过使用 0.x 版本进行沟通很有用，“这不是一个生产就绪的库，它可能随时更改，我不提供稳定性保证”。为什么我可以在那种状态下释放它？因为它可能仍然对人们有用，而发现它有用的人可能会成为贡献者。
Versioning is communication. I find it useful to communicate, through using version 0.x, "this is not a production ready library and it may change at any time, I provide no stability guarantees". Why might I release it in that state? Because it might still be useful to people, and people who find it useful may become contributors.
在 HN 中查看
moron4hire 2026/04/13 23:21
任何项目都可能随时发生变化。这就是他们从 v1 升级到 v2 的原因。但是，如果不使用完整的版本号精度，您将无法清楚地传达有关版本的信息。次要版本可能不会与以前的版本 100% 兼容，但人们仍然期望有一定程度的相似性，这样迁移就不是一件困难的任务。但是从 v0.n 到 v0.(n+1) 使用该字段来传达“地狱，任何事情都可能发生，YOLO。”

没有人关心 Chrome 的主要版本是 147。

Any project may change at any time. That's why they bump from v1 to v2. But by not using the full precision of the version number, you're not able to communicate as clearly about releases. A minor release may not be 100% compatible with the previous version, but people still expect some degree of similarity such that migrating is not a difficult task. But going from v0.n to v0.(n+1) uses that field to communicate "hell, anything could happen, YOLO."

Nobody cares that Chrome's major version is 147.

在 HN 中查看
mort96 2026/04/13 23:25
通过发布 1.0 版本的库，我传达的信息是：“我认为这个项目处于合理依赖它的状态”。

通过发布版本 0.x 的库，我传达的信息是：“我认为该项目正处于初始开发阶段，建议人们不要依赖它，除非您想参与其初始开发”。

我不明白为什么人们觉得这很困难或有争议。

By releasing a library with version 1.0, I communicate: "I consider this project to be in a state where it is reasonable to depend on it".

By releasing a library with version 0.x, I communicate: "I consider this project to be under initial development and would advice people not to depend on in unless you want to participate in its initial development".

I don't understand why people find this difficult or controversial.

在 HN 中查看
steveklabnik 2026/04/14 02:34
这里还有额外的微妙之处。

例如，有时具有 0.y 版本的项目会受到很大的依赖，因此迁移到 1.0.0 可能会非常痛苦。 Rust 中的 libc crate 就是这种情况，0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 的过渡对于生态系统来说是非常痛苦的。尽管它应该是 1.0.0 板条箱，但事实并非如此，因为导致生态系统分裂的痛苦不值得更改版本号。

There is additional subtlety here.

For example, sometimes projects that have a 0.y version get depended on a lot, and so moving to 1.0.0 can be super painful. This is the case with the libc crate in Rust, which the 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 transition was super painful for the ecosystem. Even though it should be a 1.0.0 crate, it is not, because the pain of causing an ecosystem split isn't considered to be worth the version number change.

在 HN 中查看
mort96 2026/04/14 06:22
哦嘿，我最近看到一条评论讨论了这个确切的问题： https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47752915
Oh hey I recently saw a comment which discussed this exact issue: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47752915
在 HN 中查看
steveklabnik 2026/04/14 06:44
99% 的情况下这种情况都没有问题，因为 Cargo 允许您在同一个项目中同时拥有 0.1 和 0.2 作为依赖项。它只是调用外部依赖项的包，例如 libc，它强制执行单一版本规则。
99% of the time this situation is okay, because Cargo allows you to have both 0.1 and 0.2 in the same project as dependencies. It's just packages that call out to external dependencies, like libc, where it enforces the single version rule.
在 HN 中查看
mort96 2026/04/14 07:13
你 能 在同一个项目中同时拥有 0.1 和 0.2，但你确实没有 想 到。
You can have both 0.1 and 0.2 in the same project, but you really don't want to.
在 HN 中查看
steveklabnik 2026/04/14 07:39
大多数时候，它的效果非常好，人们甚至没有注意到。

唯一遇到问题的时候是，如果您尝试将类型为 0.1 的值与采用 0.2 作为参数的函数一起使用，或者其他什么。然后你会得到一个类型错误。

Most of the time, it works so well people don't even notice.

The only time you run into a problem is if you try and use values with a type from 0.1 with a function that takes a 0.2 as an argument, or whatever. Then you get a type error.

phplovesong 2026/04/13 22:26
火狐放弃伺服了吗？我记得他们“rewrite in rust”的进度在哪里？

在 HN 中查看
dralley 2026/04/13 22:29
Firefox 整合了 Servo 的部分成果，这些成果已经成熟。 Stylo（Firefox 当前的 CSS 引擎）和 Webrender（渲染引擎）和其他一些小组件来自 Servo 项目。

当 Mozilla 决定终止对该项目的支持时，Servo 的大多数其他部分还不够成熟，无法集成，而且看起来它们在短期内也不会足够成熟。 例如，DOM 引擎当时正处于完全重写的早期阶段，因为原始版本的架构使得支持整个 Web 标准具有挑战性。

请记住，您可以继续将 Rust 添加到 Firefox，而无需替换整个组件。 Mozilla 并不是因为停止尝试从头开始重写整个组件而放弃在 Firefox 中使用更多 Rust 的想法。

Firefox incorporated parts of the Servo effort which were able to reach maturity. Stylo (Firefox's current CSS engine) and Webrender (the rendering engine) and a few other small components came from the Servo project.

Most other parts of Servo were not mature enough to integrate at the time Mozilla decided to end support for the project and didn't look like they would be mature enough any time soon. The DOM engine for example was in the early stages of being completely rewritten at the time because the original version had an architecture that made supporting the entire breadth of web standards challenging.

Keep in mind that you can continue adding Rust to Firefox without replacing whole components. It's not like Mozilla abandoned the idea of using more Rust in Firefox just because they stopped trying to rewrite whole components from the ground up.

在 HN 中查看
andruby 2026/04/13 22:34
是的，2020年8月裁员期间

Mozilla 解雇了整个 Servo 团队，但从未公开宣布这一消息。维基百科将其包含在这里： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Firefox#cite_ref-120

Yes, during the layoff of August 2020

Mozilla laid off the full Servo team, but never publicly announced this afaik. Wikipedia includes it here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Firefox#cite_ref-120

Sammi 2026/04/13 22:54
Mozilla 无法阻止自己成为自己最大的敌人。如果 Mozilla 继续致力于 Servo，Ladybird 很可能永远不会出现，而当 Mozilla 继续与开源爱好者断绝关系时，Ladybird 绝对会在成熟时超越 Firefox。
Mozilla can't help it but be their own worst enemy. Ladybird may well never have happened if Mozilla just had kept working on Servo, and Ladybird is most definitely going to out compete Firefox when it reaches maturity, as Mozilla keeps on burning bridges with open source enthusiasts.
在 HN 中查看
zarzavat 2026/04/13 23:17
Mozilla 的问题不仅是技术上的，也是文化上的。组织已经被管理者感染了。管理者们更希望保住自己的工作，而不是希望 Firefox 取得成功。显然，解决方案是经理们解雇自己，让开发人员来主持大局，但这并不会发生。

相比之下，Ladybird 是一个由开发人员主导的开源项目，没有这样的限制。他们还没有产品，但我相信几年后情况将会截然不同。

康威定律在起作用。

The problem with Mozilla is not just technical but cultural. The organization has been infected with managers. The managers want to keep their jobs more than they want Firefox to succeed. Clearly the solution is for the managers to fire themselves and allow the developers to run the show, but that was not going to happen.

Ladybird, by contrast, is a developer-lead open source project that has no such constraints. They also don't have a product yet but I'm sure the picture will be radically different in a few years.

Conway's law in action.

在 HN 中查看
estebank 2026/04/14 00:19
补充一下其他回复，Firefox 明确表示永远不会消耗所有 Servo。它始终是一个测试平台项目，其中的子项目可以迁移到 Firefox。我怀疑长期意图 可能是 让伺服达到这样的程度 可以 成为 Firefox，但这不是既定的计划。
To add to the other replies, Firefox was explicitly never going to consume all of Servo. It was always meant to be a test bed project where sub-projects could be migrated to Firefox. I suspect that the long term intent might have been for Servo to get to a point where it could become Firefox, but that wasn't the stated plan.

drzaiusx11 2026/04/13 22:12

在 HN 中查看
saghm 2026/04/13 22:20
我的理解是，Servo 的初衷是成为一种开发功能并将其移植到 Firefox 本身的方式（至少有一些功能确实发生了），而开发人员相对较慢的步伐更多是由于 Mozilla 解雇了所有从事该工作的人。 （是的，大概有很多同样的人参与其中，但我希望能够在不需要其他收入来源的情况下全职工作，最终会比需要在工作之外寻找时间并在生活中的其他事情之间取得平衡更快地取得进步，最好是避免倦怠的方式）。
My understanding is that the original intent of Servo was to be a way to develop features and port them over to Firefox itself (which did happen with at least a few features), and the relatively slower pace of developer is more due to Mozilla laying off everyone who was working on it. (Yes, presumably many of the same people are involved, but I would expect that being able to work on something full time without needing another source of income will end up making progress faster than needing to find time outside of work and balance between other things in life, ideally in a way that avoids burnout).

drzaiusx11 2026/04/13 23:09

My understanding was that from day one the desire was to make a complete "web rendering & layout engine" and only pivoted to shipping smaller sub-components like Stylo (stylesheets) when it appeared to be "taking too long." I followed the project from the early days through the layoffs, but I may be misremembering things.

mort96 2026/04/13 23:02

在 HN 中查看
8NNTt8z3QvLT8tp 2026/04/13 23:26
不过，对于没有 JIT 的安全优势，还是有话要说的。特别是如果您使用 Rust 作为引擎，您应该拥有相当可靠的安全性。
There's something to be said for the security benefits of not having a JIT though. Especially if you've used Rust for the engine you should have pretty solid security.
在 HN 中查看
px43 2026/04/14 00:09
是的，从安全角度来看，拥有可写和可执行的代码部分是一个巨大的禁忌。一般来说，JIT 是一个根本上不安全的概念。根据定义，它是以安全换取速度。
Yeah, having a code section that is writable and executable is a huge no-no from a security standpoint. JIT is a fundamentally insecure concept, just in general. By definition it's trading security for speed.

在 HN 中查看
mort96 2026/04/13 23:14
该页面说：

> 从源代码解析到字节码执行的完整 JavaScript 执行管道。

所以它是一个字节码解释器，而不是 JIT。

它可能仍然可以用于大量用例的生产。我可以将它用作某些可插入软件或游戏的脚本层。我认为它不适合用于与 Firefox 和 Chrome 竞争的“生产就绪的网络浏览器”。

编辑：另外由于某种原因它的所有组件都被称为 v8_something？这太令人反感了，你不能就这样取另一个项目的名字……而且从作者的 Reddit 评论来看，它似乎主要是人工智能的废话。我猜克劳德在网站上写了“生产就绪”部分，我不相信它。

That page says:

> Complete JavaScript execution pipeline from source code parsing to bytecode execution.

So it's a bytecode interpreter, not a JIT.

It might still be production ready for a bunch of use cases. I may use it as a scripting layer for some pluggable piece of software or a game. I wouldn't consider it appropriate for a "production ready web browser" which intends to compete with Firefox and Chrome.

EDIT: Also for some reason all its components are called v8_something? That's pretty off putting, you can't just take another project's name like that.. and from the author's Reddit comments it seems to be mostly AI slop anyway. I'm guessing Claude wrote the "production ready" part on the website, I wouldn't trust it.

bastawhiz 2026/04/13 22:14
二十五年前，系统 Web 视图可作为 VB6 中的拖放组件使用。作为一个概念，这并没有什么“新”的，并且有很多理由不想使用 Blink/WebKit。
System web views were available as drag and drop components in VB6 two and a half decades ago. There's nothing "new" about that as a concept, and plenty of reasons to not want to use Blink/WebKit.

diath 2026/04/13 22:29
> 二十五年前，系统 Web 视图可作为 VB6 中的拖放组件使用。作为一个概念，这并没有什么“新”之处

我们在讨论 Rust 库的线程中，从逻辑上讲，我指的是 Rust 空间中 GUI 渲染的当前方法（例如 Tauri 和 Dioxus）。

> 并且有很多理由不想使用 Blink/WebKit。

例如？您能否列举一些反对 Blink/WebKit（技术）的客观原因，但不涉及不喜欢 Google/Apple？

> System web views were available as drag and drop components in VB6 two and a half decades ago. There's nothing "new" about that as a concept

We are in a thread discussing a Rust library, logically, I was referring to the current approach in GUI rendering in the Rust space (such as Tauri and Dioxus).

> and plenty of reasons to not want to use Blink/WebKit.

Such as? Can you name a few objective reasons against Blink/WebKit (the technology) that does not involve just not liking Google/Apple?

在 HN 中查看
bastawhiz 2026/04/14 02:31
> Rust 空间中 GUI 渲染的当前方法（例如 Tauri 和 Dioxus）。

Tauri 本身不渲染 Web 视图。它在幕后使用了wry。 Dioxus 根本不是一个网络视图，它应该有一个根本不同的目的。

> 您能否列举一些反对 Blink/WebKit（技术）的客观原因，但不涉及不喜欢 Google/Apple？

如果您有一个跨平台应用程序，则必须根据可用的引擎及其年龄来担心哪些功能有效或无效，这很糟糕。您也不知道是否注入了影响体验的用户脚本。调试是不可能的，许多用户甚至不知道正在使用什么浏览器引擎，他们只知道您的应用程序无法运行。

如果您为 Servo 构建，它在每个平台上的工作原理都完全相同。你可以使用 wry 来测试 Edge 在 Windows 上是否良好，WebKit 在 Macos 的过去几个版本上是否有效，gtk WebKit 是否有效，等等，或者你也可以只使用 Servo。

更不用说，Servo 可能比用户在引擎盖下安装的任何铬口味都要轻得多。

> the current approach in GUI rendering in the Rust space (such as Tauri and Dioxus).

Tauri itself doesn't render web views. It uses wry under the hood. Dioxus isn't a web view at all and deserves a fundamentally different purpose.

> Can you name a few objective reasons against Blink/WebKit (the technology) that does not involve just not liking Google/Apple?

If you have a cross platform application, it sucks having to worry about which features work or don't work based on which engine is available and how old it is. You also don't know if there are user scripts being injected that are affecting the experience. It's impossible to debug and many users don't even know what browser engine is being used, they just know your app doesn't work.

If you build for Servo, it works exactly the same on every platform. You could use wry and test that Edge is good on Windows, WebKit works on the past few versions of Macos, gtk WebKit works, etc etc, or you can just use Servo.

Not to mention, Servo is probably much lighter than whatever flavor of chromium the user has installed under the hood.

swiftcoder 2026/04/13 22:13

在 HN 中查看
chrismorgan 2026/04/13 23:07
Linux（GNU/Linux 或其他）甚至没有 概念 系统 Web 视图。最接近这个概念的可能是 WebKitGTK，它可能是 侏儒 系统 Web 视图的想法，但就流行度或可用性而言，它与 macOS 上的 WebKit 或 Windows 上的 WebView2（或过去的 MSHTML）完全不同。

作为除 gnome-shell 之外的桌面环境的用户，我只安装了 webkitgtk-6.0，因为我选择安装 Epiphany — 它是在 Safari 上进行测试的一个很好的代理，而 Apple 使 Safari 的价格高得离谱。

Linux (GNU/Linux or whatever) doesn’t even have the concept of a system web view. The closest you might get to the notion is probably WebKitGTK which is perhaps the GNOME idea of a system web view, but it’s nothing like WebKit on macOS or WebView2 (or MSHTML in the past) on Windows for popularity or availability.

As a user of a desktop environment other than gnome-shell, I only have webkitgtk-6.0 installed because I chose to install Epiphany—it’s a good proxy for testing on Safari, which Apple makes ridiculously expensive.

charcircuit 2026/04/14 00:08
那不是元。元数据将通过 Blink 发布，因此您只需支持单个 Web 引擎的单个版本，而不是许多不同 Web 引擎的多个版本。
That is not the meta. The meta is to ship blink so you only have to support a single version of a single web engine in stead of many versions of many different web engines.
